description: Minimum rules for peaceful coexistence and interaction between persons. Drawn from general legal and ethical traditions: reciprocity (Kant, Rawls), social contract theory (Locke, Rousseau), and international norms.

entries.statement: Treat others as you would wish to be treated in the same circumstances.
entries.source: Categorical Imperative (Kant, Groundwork 1785); Golden Rule across moral traditions

entries.statement: Any sexual activity must be consensual, freely entered, and reversible at any time by either party.
entries.source: Istanbul Convention (2011) Art. 36; general legal standard

entries.statement: Taking, damaging, or destroying another person's property without consent is prohibited.
entries.source: Locke, Two Treatises on Government (1689); general property law

entries.statement: In interactions where accuracy matters, intentional deception harms the other party and the social fabric.
entries.source: Kant, On a Supposed Right to Lie; Parfit, Reasons and Persons (1984)

entries.statement: Using threats, intimidation, or coercion to control another person's behaviour violates their autonomy.
entries.source: UDHR Art. 3; general principle of autonomy in ethics

entries.statement: Every person is entitled to a domain of personal information and space that others must not intrude upon without consent.
entries.source: UDHR Art. 12; GDPR Recital 1 (2016); Warren & Brandeis (1890)

entries.statement: Spreading false statements of fact about a person with intent to harm their reputation is prohibited.
Disputes between persons should be resolved through peaceful dialogue or legitimate legal channels, not through violence.
entries.source: UN Charter Art. 2(3); general principle of rule of law

entries.statement: When a person is in immediate danger and help is possible without unreasonable risk, providing that help is the baseline of human solidarity.
entries.source: Duty-to-rescue statutes (many jurisdictions); Kant, doctrine of imperfect duties
